query: As of early July 2026, what percentage of Russia's national oil-refining capacity is offline/disabled due to Ukrainian drone strikes?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of findings:

## Summary: Russian Oil-Refining Capacity Offline (Early July 2026)

**The answer depends on the source and methodology, ranging from ~20% to nearly 50%, with most estimates clustering around one-third to just over 40%.**

**Key estimates:**
- **42.7%** — Ukraine's General Staff (July 4, 2026), an official belligerent claim (upper bound)
- **20%–40%** — Financial Times, aggregating independent analyst estimates (July 9)
- **~50% ("nearly half")** — Energy Intelligence: >3.1M b/d of 6.6M b/d fleet down (July 9)
- **~one-third** — Tech Times, late June 2026

**Objective throughput anchor:** Russian refineries processed just **3.91 million b/d in July 2026** — the lowest since March 2005 (21+ years), down 1.4M b/d year-over-year (Bloomberg/EA Analytics). Against ~6.6M b/d nominal capacity, that's utilization near 55–60%.

**Important caveat:** "Disabled capacity" (physical damage, some repaired) differs from sustained throughput lost. Bloomberg's tally shows Ukraine likely struck 24 of Russia's 34 large refineries in ~50 attacks over 100 days. Carnegie (June 2026) framed the sector as "battered but not broken."

**Most defensible single figure:** ~30–40% offline, within a full plausible range of 20% to ~47%.

The structured output has been delivered with 12 key facts, 12 data points, and 10 sources.

sources.title: Ukrainian Defense Forces Disable 43% of Russia's Total Oil Refining Capacity
sources.snippet: Ukraine's General Staff announced strikes have disabled 42.7% of Russia's total oil refining capacity, inflicting an estimated $13.5 billion in losses since August 2025.

sources.title: Ukrainian drones knock out up to 40% of Russia's oil refining capacity - FT
sources.snippet: FT analyst estimates: drone strikes may have knocked out between 20% and 40% of Russia's oil refining capacity; June processing ~4.1M b/d, 35% below nominal capacity.

sources.title: Russia's Downstream Crisis Ripples Through Global Markets
sources.snippet: Nearly half of Russia's 6.6 million b/d refining capacity knocked offline; more than 3.1 million b/d currently down. Published July 9, 2026.

sources.title: Oil Refining in Russia Has Fallen to a Two-Decade Low
sources.snippet: July 2026 processing at 3.91M b/d, lowest since March 2005; down 1.4M b/d YoY. Cites Bloomberg/EA Analytics (Energy Aspects), July 13, 2026.

sources.title: Ukraine strikes all 10 of Russia's largest oil refineries in 2026, gutting up to 42% of refining capacity
sources.snippet: At its peak, damage to Russian refining capacity estimated between 20% and 42%; all 10 largest refineries struck in 2026.

sources.title: Ukraine Drone Blitz Burns Moscow Refinery: One-Third of Russian Refining Goes Offline
sources.snippet: Approximately one-third of Russia's national refining capacity offline as of late June 2026 after June 18 drone offensive on Moscow.

sources.title: Russian Oil Refining Slumps to Lowest Level Since 2005
sources.snippet: Refining ~3.91M b/d, down 1.4M b/d YoY; Bloomberg tally: Ukraine likely struck at least 24 of Russia's 34 large refineries in 50 attacks over 100 days.

sources.title: 2025-2026 Russian fuel crisis
sources.snippet: By July 2026 most Russian regions faced fuel restrictions affecting ~50 million people (~35% of population); Euro-5 to Euro-3 gasoline decree signed.

sources.title: Russian Oil Sector Battered but Not Broken by Ukrainian Air Attacks
sources.snippet: Carnegie analysis (June 2026) cautioning the sector is battered but not broken, against overstating permanent damage.

sources.title: Bloomberg: Russia's oil refining falls to lowest level in 21 years amid Ukrainian strikes
sources.snippet: Russian refineries processed avg 3.91M b/d in July per EA Analytics/Energy Aspects, lowest since March 2005.

data_points.metric: Russia oil refining capacity disabled (Ukrainian General Staff claim)
Russia refining capacity deprived (FT analyst estimate, low end)
Russia refining capacity deprived (FT analyst estimate, high end)
Russia refining capacity offline (Energy Intelligence, 'nearly half')
Russian refining capacity currently down (Energy Intelligence)
Russian crude processing rate July 2026 (lowest since March 2005)
